description: src/lib.rs:100 calls parsed.path().split_once('/') on a SecretUri path that has already been percent-decoded by SecretUri::parse() (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1157). If a Bitwarden project name contains a literal slash, the user must percent-encode it as %2F, but after decode the slash is indistinguishable from the project/secret separator. A URI 'secretx:bitwarden:proj%2Fa/secret' decodes to path 'proj/a/secret', and split_once('/') splits at the first slash, yielding project='proj' and secret='a/secret' — wrong. Two reasonable fixes: (a) split BEFORE percent-decode (would require a SecretUri API change to expose the raw path), or (b) split on the LAST slash (rsplit_once) so the secret name can contain slashes but the project name cannot, which is asymmetric but probably matches Bitwarden semantics better since secret keys are more likely to contain '/'. (c) Document the limitation explicitly and reject paths with multiple slashes after decode. Investigate whether Bitwarden allows '/' in project/secret names; if it doesn't, downgrade to P3 docs-only.
title: split_once('/') on percent-decoded path mishandles names containing literal slash
